question: What does XML stand for?
There is a way of describing XML data, how?
options: XML uses a description node to describe data
XML uses XSL to describe data
answer: XML uses a DTD to describe the data

question: XML's goal is to replace HTML

Is this a "well formed" XML document?

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<note>
<to>Tove</to>
<from>Jani</from>
<heading>Reminder</heading>
<body>Don't forget me this weekend!</body>
</note>

Is this a "well formed" XML document?

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<to>Tove</to>
<from>Jani</from>

Which statement is true?
options: All XML elements must be lower case
All XML documents must have a DTD
answer: All XML elements must be properly closed

question: Which statement is NOT true?
options: XML elements must be properly nested
XML documents must have a root tag
answer: White-space is not preserved in XML

question: XML preserves white spaces

Is this a "well formed" XML document?

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<note>
<to age="29">Tove</to>
<from>Jani</from>
</note>

Is this a "well formed" XML document?

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<note>
<to age=29>Tove</to>
<from>Jani</from>
</note>

options: Attributes must occur in defined order

question: What are XML entities used for?
options: Entities define shortcuts to standard elements
Entities define shortcuts to standard attributes
answer: Entities define shortcuts to standard text or special characters
